summary: Iteration 5 end-to-end verification of society settings, directory, committee, flats summary, and complaint email notification. Backend: 10/10 pytest cases pass (GET/PATCH /api/society RBAC, GET /api/directory, GET /api/committee, GET /api/flats/summary shape+math, complaint PATCH triggers 'Complaint update' email log). Frontend: sidebar-society-name + header-society-name render 'Maintyn Society'; admin sees sidebar-society-settings and Directory nav; Society Settings dialog pre-fills, saves to 'Green Valley Residency', sidebar updates immediately via SocietyContext refresh, then restored to 'Maintyn Society'. /app/directory renders 3 committee cards and 23 directory rows; directory-search filters correctly ('admin' -> 1 row). /app/flats renders block-filter-all, occupancy-filter-all, 8 block-filter buttons, 7 block-section groups, all 5 StatCard labels visible; clicking block-filter-A narrows to 1 section. /app/complaints: banner correctly hidden for admin (residents-only), complaint-card and complaint-status-chip data-testids resolve after fixing Chip prop forwarding. Society name restored to default.

minor.issue: contact_email validator rejects .test / .local TLDs (pydantic email-validator strictness). Consider EmailStr with check_deliverability=False for internal fields, or document allowed TLDs.
Endpoint exposes email + phone of all users to any authenticated user including residents. Consider privacy toggle (opt-out) or masking phone for non-committee viewers.

ui_bugs.issue: Chip did not forward data-testid (or any extra props) — status-chip test hooks were invisible to automation. FIXED by spreading ...rest onto the span.

issues: Radix DialogContent missing aria-describedby (React console warning; existing app-wide a11y issue, non-blocking).
Sidebar sub-line 'ON MAINTYN · COMMUNITY …' is truncated / ellipsized on 260px sidebar — the '…' cuts mid-word rather than after a whole token. Cosmetic.

action_items: Ship the Chip data-testid pass-through fix (already applied in /app/frontend/src/components/Shared.jsx) — no further action needed unless main agent prefers a different pattern.
Consider masking phone/email in /api/directory for resident viewers (privacy).
Consider relaxing email validator for society.contact_email or documenting the .test/.local restriction.
critical_code_review_comments: Chip component in Shared.jsx did NOT forward props — any consumer passing className/aria/data-testid was silently dropped. Fixed via {...rest} spread on the span. Audit other Shared.jsx primitives (EmptyState, StatCard, PageHeader) for the same anti-pattern.
server.py is now ~1454 lines — over the 700-line guideline. Split into routers/modules (society.py, directory.py, complaints.py, flats.py) for maintainability.
/api/directory has no pagination — will be slow at scale (>1000 users). Add limit/offset or search-on-server.

SocietyContext refetches on every user change; that is fine, but if PATCH /api/society response is used directly the sidebar could update without a second GET — currently there is a 2-request flash on save. Optimize by consuming PATCH response.
Complaint status-change email is fire-and-forget within the request; if SMTP is slow, PATCH latency spikes. Move to BackgroundTasks or asyncio.create_task.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (all data-testids resolve after Chip fix; flows verified)

seed_data_creation: Registered one throwaway resident test_res_it5_<ts>_<pid>@example.com for resident-side RBAC assertions. Created a fallback complaint 'TEST it5 notify' via admin when no non-admin complaint existed. Society name PATCHed to 'Green Valley Residency' and restored to 'Maintyn Society' at the end of the test.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Society doc is singleton in db.society with id='singleton', current name 'Maintyn Society'. Directory endpoint is public to any authenticated user. Chip component (Shared.jsx) now forwards props — reuse this for any future test hooks. Pydantic email-validator disallows .test TLD, use .example.com for test emails. For xdist-parallel tests that register users, seed emails with pid+ms to avoid dup collisions.
